sentiment_score_definition: x <= -0.35: Bearish; -0.35 < x <= -0.15: Somewhat-Bearish; -0.15 < x < 0.15: Neutral; 0.15 <= x < 0.35: Somewhat_Bullish; x >= 0.35: Bullish
relevance_score_definition: 0 < x <= 1, with a higher score indicating higher relevance.

feed.title: Madrigal Pharmaceuticals Signs Global siRNA Licensing Deal With Ribo
feed.summary: Madrigal Pharmaceuticals Inc. has announced a global licensing agreement with Suzhou Ribo Life Science Co. Ltd. to develop six preclinical small interfering RNA programs targeting metabolic dysfunction-associated steatohepatitis (MASH). The deal includes an upfront payment of $60 million to Ribo, with potential milestone payments up to $4.4 billion plus royalties. This agreement expands Madrigal's pipeline beyond its approved MASH therapy, Rezdiffra, aiming to provide genetically targeted treatment approaches for the liver disease.

feed.title: B of A Securities Upgrades Hexcel (HXL)
feed.summary: B of A Securities upgraded its outlook for Hexcel (HXL) from Underperform to Neutral on February 12, 2026. Despite the upgrade, the average one-year price target for Hexcel suggests a 4.20% downside from its latest closing price. The company projects an annual revenue increase of 13.21% and an annual non-GAAP EPS of 5.31.

feed.title: Short Interest in Middlefield Banc Corp. (NASDAQ:MBCN) Decreases By 13.2%
feed.summary: Middlefield Banc Corp. (NASDAQ:MBCN) experienced a 13.2% decrease in short interest in January, with total short interest falling to 32,355 shares by January 30th. The company's stock traded up $0.23 on Tuesday, reaching $34.95, and it recently announced a quarterly dividend of $0.21 per share. Middlefield Banc reported Q4 earnings of $0.38 per share, missing consensus estimates, and analytical ratings currently average a "Hold" with a consensus target price of $35.00.

feed.title: Kimco Realty Corp stock hits 52-week high at $22.99
feed.summary: Kimco Realty Corp (KIM) stock has reached a 52-week high of $22.99, demonstrating a 9.26% total return over the past year and a 12.04% year-to-date gain. The real estate investment trust boasts a strong Piotroski score of 8 and a 4.58% dividend yield, maintaining dividend payments for 35 consecutive years. This milestone follows a strong Q4 2025 performance, exceeding EPS and revenue expectations, and a price target increase from Stifel to $25.75 with a Buy rating.

feed.title: Kosmos Energy Ltd. (NYSE:KOS) Short Interest Down 13.6% in January
feed.summary: Kosmos Energy Ltd. (NYSE:KOS) experienced a 13.6% decline in short interest in January, with total short interest reaching 50,239,989 shares. This represents 10.7% of the company's float and a 3.5 days-to-cover ratio. Despite insider selling by CEO Andrew G. Inglis and SVP Josh R. Marion, institutional ownership remains high at 95.33%, with firms like Equinox and Goldman Sachs increasing their positions.

feed.title: Coterra Energy stock hits 52-week high at 31.68 USD
feed.summary: Coterra Energy Inc. stock has reached a new 52-week high of $31.68 USD, reflecting a 12.48% increase over the past year and a 31.65% gain in the last six months. The company, which maintains a P/E ratio of 14.49 and a 2.81% dividend yield with 36 consecutive years of dividend payments, is considered undervalued by InvestingPro analysis. This milestone follows news of an all-stock merger deal with Devon Energy, creating a major U.S. shale operator, and a reiterated Buy rating from UBS with a price target of $33.

feed.title: Atmos Energy stock hits all-time high at 180.93 USD
feed.summary: Atmos Energy Corp (ATO) has reached an all-time high of $180.93 per share, surpassing its previous 52-week high, demonstrating strong investor confidence and a 25.1% total return over the past year. Despite a slight revenue miss in Q1 fiscal year 2026, the company exceeded EPS expectations and has a consistent 44-year dividend payment history. This performance highlights Atmos Energy's resilience and growth within the energy sector, attracting significant market attention.

feed.title: Former ABS CEO Wiernicki Joins Royal Caribbean’s Board of Directors
feed.summary: Royal Caribbean Group has appointed Christopher J. Wiernicki, former Chairman and CEO of American Bureau of Shipping (ABS), to its Board of Directors. Wiernicki brings over four decades of marine and offshore experience, with expertise in operations, infrastructure, and safety management. Chairman and CEO Jason Liberty noted Wiernicki's disciplined approach to growth, innovation, and responsible operations will be highly valuable to the company's evolution.

feed.title: Argus Adjusts Price Target on Expedia Group to $250 From $345, Maintains Buy Rating
feed.summary: Argus has adjusted its price target for Expedia Group (EXPE) from $345 to $250 while maintaining a Buy rating on the stock. This adjustment reflects a revised outlook from the financial analyst firm. The article also lists several other analyst rating changes for Expedia Group on the same date and in the days prior, indicating significant analyst activity around the company.

feed.title: Scorpions mark 60 years with 2026 Las Vegas residency at Planet Hollywood
feed.summary: The iconic German rock band Scorpions will return to Las Vegas for a new headlining residency at PH Live at Planet Hollywood Resort & Casino from September 17 to October 3, 2026. This residency, celebrating the band’s 60th anniversary, will feature special guest Buckcherry and follows three previous sold-out runs. Tickets go on sale Saturday, February 21, with presales beginning earlier in the week.

feed.title: BMO Capital Adjusts Welltower Price Target to $240 From $225, Maintains Outperform Rating
feed.summary: BMO Capital has raised its price target for Welltower Inc. (WELL) to $240 from $225, while reaffirming an Outperform rating on the stock. This adjustment reflects continued positive sentiment from analysts towards the healthcare real estate investment trust. Other firms like UBS and Cantor Fitzgerald have also recently increased their price targets for Welltower, maintaining Buy and Overweight ratings, respectively, suggesting broad analyst confidence in the company's future performance.

feed.title: HSBC Adjusts Price Target on AbbVie to $275 From $265, Maintains Buy Rating
feed.summary: HSBC has increased its price target for AbbVie (ABBV) to $275 from $265, while reiterating a "Buy" rating on the stock. This adjustment reflects a positive outlook from the analytical firm regarding the pharmaceutical company's future performance.

feed.title: Citizens to acquire downstream energy adviser Matrix Capital Markets Group
feed.summary: Citizens Financial Group Inc. is set to acquire substantially all assets of Matrix Capital Markets Group Inc., an advisory firm specializing in the downstream energy and convenience retail sector. This acquisition, expected to close in Q1 2026, aims to expand Citizens' advisory capabilities and client offerings by integrating Matrix's expertise in M&A transactions within these specialized sectors. The transaction will be funded with cash, and Matrix will operate as a division of Citizens JMP Securities LLC (CJMPS) upon closing.

feed.title: ExxonMobil to Speak at Morgan Stanley Energy & Power Conference
feed.summary: Exxon Mobil Corporation announced that Jack Williams, Senior Vice President, will participate in a fireside chat at the Morgan Stanley Energy & Power Conference on March 3, 2026, at 11:00 a.m. ET. A live webcast will be available, with an archived version accessible on the ExxonMobil website approximately 24 hours after the event. The article also provides information about ExxonMobil's business, its greenhouse gas emission-reduction plans, and recent financial news.

feed.title: NorthCoast Research Adjusts Tyler Technologies Price Target to $430 From $600, Maintains Buy Rating
feed.summary: NorthCoast Research has adjusted its price target for Tyler Technologies (TYL) to $430, down from $600, while reiterating a Buy rating on the stock. This adjustment places NorthCoast Research among several other firms that have recently lowered their price targets for Tyler Technologies, including Oppenheimer, Needham, TD Cowen, Stifel, Loop Capital, Goldman Sachs, Barclays, Piper Sandler, JPMorgan, Wolfe Research, Cantor Fitzgerald, Evercore ISI, and Truist Securities. Despite these reductions in price targets, many analysts, including NorthCoast Research, continue to maintain a "Buy" or "Overweight" rating for the software company, which specializes in information management software for the public sector.

feed.title: Pfizer's Cancer Drug Combo Shows Strong Results In Key Colorecatal Cancer Trial
feed.summary: Pfizer announced strong results for its BRAFTOVI regimen in treating metastatic colorectal cancer, showing statistically significant improvement in progression-free survival. The regimen achieved a 60.9% objective response rate compared to 40.0% for traditional treatments in the BREAKWATER trial. Despite these positive clinical results, Pfizer's stock currently shows mixed momentum, trading below its short-term moving averages but near its 52-week high, with analysts generally holding a neutral rating.

feed.title: NorthCoast Research Adjusts Generac Price Target to $247 From $194, Maintains Buy Rating
feed.summary: NorthCoast Research has increased its price target for Generac Holdings (GNRC) to $247, up from the previous $194, while reiterating a Buy rating on the stock. This adjustment reflects an updated positive outlook from the research firm regarding the power generation solutions company. The article also lists several other analyst rating changes for Generac from mid-February, with most maintaining Buy or Overweight ratings and raising price targets.

feed.title: Autonomous Research Adjusts Price Target on Everest Group to $333 From $327, Maintains Underperform Rating
feed.summary: Autonomous Research has adjusted its price target for Everest Group (EG) to $333, an increase from the previous $327, while maintaining an "Underperform" rating on the stock. This update comes amidst several recent analyst adjustments, with other firms like BMO Capital, Mizuho, Jefferies, Wells Fargo, and Cantor Fitzgerald also revising their price targets and ratings on Everest Group in February. The company recently reported its Q4 2025 earnings, swinging to a quarterly profit on higher investment returns despite a decline in revenue.

feed.title: Leidos tops profit forecasts but revenue miss weighs on shares
feed.summary: This article does not contain enough information to generate a summary. The provided content is only "MSN", which is insufficient for summarizing.

feed.title: Pfizer's (PFE) Braftovi Shows Promise in Colorectal Cancer Trial
feed.summary: Pfizer's Braftovi, in combination with cetuximab and Folfiri, has demonstrated promising results in late-stage trials for colorectal cancer treatment, particularly for patients with a BRAF V600E mutation. While Pfizer Inc. maintains a strong market presence and operational efficiency, financial analysis reveals declining revenue growth and an Altman Z-Score indicating potential financial stress. The company aims for FDA approval for this combination regimen based on the trial's success.

feed.title: Blackstone-Backed Ad Tech Firm Liftoff Withdraws IPO Filing
feed.summary: Liftoff Mobile Inc., a mobile advertising platform backed by Blackstone Inc., has withdrawn its IPO filing. The company had previously postponed plans to raise up to $762 million. The decision comes amidst a challenging market for tech stocks.

feed.title: Stifel Adjusts Price Target on Martin Marietta Materials to $712 From $703, Maintains Buy Rating
feed.summary: Stifel has increased its price target for Martin Marietta Materials (MLM) to $712 from $703, while reaffirming a Buy rating on the stock. This adjustment comes amidst other analyst actions on the company, reflecting ongoing evaluations of its financial outlook and market position. Martin Marietta Materials is a leading producer of aggregates and construction materials in the US.

feed.title: BofA Securities Upgrades Equifax and TransUnion Ratings
feed.summary: BofA Securities has upgraded its ratings for Equifax (EFX) and TransUnion (TRU), citing a positive outlook for the business and information services sector, with expectations of significant revenue, EPS, and free cash flow growth in 2026. Both companies meet the criteria for robust information services firms, possessing proprietary data and high switching costs, with Equifax's EWS segment poised for substantial growth. Following the upgrades, both EFX and TRU shares saw positive movement in pre-market trading, reflecting strong investor confidence.

feed.title: Conagra Brands Reiterates Full-Year Guidance Ahead of Conference
feed.summary: Conagra Brands has reiterated its full-year fiscal year 2026 guidance, expecting organic net sales growth between -1% and +1%, an adjusted operating margin of 11.0% to 11.5%, and adjusted EPS between $1.70 and $1.85. The company also improved its free cash flow conversion estimate to approximately 100% from 90%. This reiteration of guidance comes ahead of an upcoming conference, with analysts like Wells Fargo and UBS adjusting their price targets for CAG.

feed.title: Wells Fargo upgrades Jack Henry stock rating on revenue growth outlook
feed.summary: Wells Fargo has upgraded Jack Henry & Associates (NASDAQ:JKHY) to Overweight from Equal Weight, raising its price target to $196.00 due to strong conviction in the company's fiscal 2027 revenue acceleration. The firm anticipates growth from new core banking, digital, and payments solutions, improved renewal pricing, and positive card trends, while dismissing AI-related stock pullbacks as unfounded. Jack Henry also reported strong Q2 2026 results and increased its quarterly dividend, with another firm, Baird, also upgrading its stock rating.

feed.title: Jefferies Adjusts Newmont Price Target to $158 From $136, Maintains Buy Rating
feed.summary: Jefferies has increased its price target for Newmont Corporation (NEM) to $158 from $136, while reiterating a "Buy" rating on the stock. This adjustment reflects a positive outlook on the gold mining company. Newmont Corporation, a leading gold mine exploration and operation company, reported 6.5 million ounces of gold sold in 2024.

feed.title: Caesars Entertainment earnings up next: Can Vegas stabilize?
feed.summary: Caesars Entertainment (CZR) is set to release its fourth-quarter earnings report, with analysts expecting a narrower loss and a slight increase in revenue compared to the previous quarter. The company faces challenges from declining Las Vegas visitation and high debt levels, but some analysts foresee an improvement in regional casino performance and Vegas results due to seasonal factors. Investors will focus on Las Vegas demand stabilization, regional casino trends, the digital segment's performance, and management's plans for debt reduction.

feed.title: BofA reinstates Fair Isaac stock rating at Buy on pricing power
feed.summary: BofA Securities reinstated a Buy rating on Fair Isaac Corp. (NYSE:FICO), setting a price target of $1,900, which suggests a 39% upside from its current price. The positive outlook is attributed to FICO's strong pricing power from its FICO Score product, high gross profit margins, and robust revenue growth. The company's Mortgage Direct Licensing program and strengthening software business are identified as key growth drivers.

feed.title: Zahn Dental Launches Zirlux Independence ML
feed.summary: Zahn Dental, a division of Henry Schein, Inc., has introduced Zirlux Independence ML, new multi-layered zirconia discs manufactured in the USA. These discs are designed to meet dental laboratories' demand for strength, superior esthetics, and efficiency, featuring consistent shades, natural multilayer esthetics, and full-disc milling capabilities. The product aims to streamline the milling process, improve production, and increase profitability for dental labs with its balance of strength and esthetics.

feed.title: BMO Capital Adjusts Hilton Worldwide PT to $325 From $320, Maintains Market Perform Rating
feed.summary: BMO Capital has increased its price target for Hilton Worldwide Holdings Inc. (HLT) to $325 from $320, while reiterating a "Market Perform" rating on the stock. This adjustment reflects a modest upward revision in the analyst's valuation for the hospitality company. The article also lists other recent analyst adjustments for Hilton, with varying price targets and ratings.

feed.title: Insights Ahead: Global Payments's Quarterly Earnings
feed.summary: Global Payments (NYSE: GPN) is set to release its quarterly earnings on February 18, 2026, with analysts forecasting an EPS of $3.12. Investors are keen to see if the company surpasses estimates and provides positive guidance, which can significantly influence stock performance. The company's shares have been down 34.52% over the last 52 weeks, making this earnings report crucial for long-term shareholders.

feed.title: Charting a New Course: Inside the 2026 Resurgence of Norwegian Cruise Line Holdings (NCLH)
feed.summary: Norwegian Cruise Line Holdings (NCLH) has entered a transformative chapter in 2026, marked by a 6.26% stock rally following a leadership change and a significant 17-ship expansion agreement. Despite its substantial $14.4 billion debt, the company is poised for growth with record-breaking bookings and a focus on operational excellence under new CEO John W. Chidsey. NCLH's "three-brand" strategy and upcoming fleet innovations position it for a high-reward play in the resilient cruise sector, though investor sentiment remains a "Moderate Buy" due to leverage concerns.

feed.title: Arm & Hammer class action alleges baby detergent falsely advertised as hypoallergenic
feed.summary: A new class action lawsuit has been filed against Church & Dwight Co. Inc., alleging that their Arm & Hammer hypoallergenic baby detergent products are falsely advertised. Plaintiff Davontaya Johnson claims the detergents contain fragrance chemicals that can cause allergic reactions, leading consumers to pay a premium under false pretenses. The lawsuit seeks to represent a California class of consumers and demands various forms of relief.

feed.title: Earnings Outlook For CF Industries Holdings
feed.summary: CF Industries Holdings (NYSE: CF) is set to release its quarterly earnings report on Wednesday, 2026-02-18, with analysts expecting an EPS of $2.41. The company's past performance shows that beating EPS estimates can sometimes lead to a share price drop, as seen with a 4.23% decrease after the last beat. Shares are up 11.39% over the last 52 weeks, and investors are looking for strong guidance for the upcoming quarter.

feed.title: Booking Holdings to Make Fourth Quarter and Full Year 2025 Earnings Press Release Available on Company's Investor Relations Website on February 18
feed.summary: Booking Holdings (NASDAQ: BKNG) announced that its fourth quarter and full year 2025 financial results will be released via press release on February 18 at approximately 4:00 p.m. ET. The press release will be available on the company's Investor Relations website. A conference call to discuss these results will follow at 4:30 p.m. ET on the same day, accessible via webcast.

feed.title: Targa Resources stock hits all-time high at 225.91 USD
feed.summary: Targa Resources Inc. (TRGP) stock has reached an all-time high of $225.91, reflecting significant investor confidence and strong performance in the energy sector. The company's stock has increased by 11.58% over the past year and 21.95% year-to-date, with a market capitalization of $48.06 billion. Recent positive developments include the acquisition of Stakeholder Midstream for $1.25 billion and several analyst price target increases, though InvestingPro suggests the stock may be slightly overvalued.

feed.title: Options Exercise Activity Initiated By Christopher Knutson At Graco Worth $0
feed.summary: Christopher Knutson, EVP at Graco (NYSE: GGG), initiated an options exercise activity by executing stock options for 0 shares of GGG. This was disclosed in an SEC Form 4 filing on February 17, with the options having an exercise price of $0.0 per share. Despite this, the article also details Graco's positive financial health, including a 9.16% revenue growth and a low debt-to-equity ratio, while noting its shares were down by 1.07% at $93.66 on the morning of the disclosure.

feed.title: Humana (NYSE:HUM) Price Target Lowered to $200.00 at Truist Financial
feed.summary: Truist Financial has lowered its price target for Humana (NYSE:HUM) from $285.00 to $200.00, maintaining a "hold" rating, which suggests an 8.32% upside from its current price. This revision follows several other firms, including Goldman Sachs and Leerink, also cutting their price targets for the insurance provider. Despite these adjustments, Humana slightly surpassed quarterly EPS estimates and reported robust revenue growth, and the company set its FY2026 guidance at 9.00 EPS.

feed.title: Deep Dive: HCA Healthcare Navigates Policy Shifts and Market Volatility
feed.summary: HCA Healthcare, a dominant player in the American for-profit hospital sector, experienced a 4% stock retreat, sparking debate among investors. This dip occurred amidst post-pandemic volume surges, a cooling labor crisis, and impending federal healthcare policy changes. Despite record revenues and aggressive capital returns, the market is weighing HCA's operational excellence against these shifting regulatory tides, prompting a deep dive into whether this decline signals structural headwinds or is merely a transient correction.

feed.title: FactSet Research Systems (NYSE:FDS) Given New $195.00 Price Target at Wells Fargo & Company
feed.summary: Wells Fargo & Company has lowered its price target for FactSet Research Systems (NYSE:FDS) to $195.00 from $215.00, maintaining an "underweight" rating. This new target suggests a potential downside of 1.52% from its prior closing price. Despite FactSet beating quarterly EPS estimates and authorizing a $600 million share buyback, the overall analyst sentiment is mixed to negative, with a consensus rating of "Reduce" and an average price target of $298.54.

feed.title: Kratos (KTOS) Secures Ground Segment Contract for OmanSat-1 Proj
feed.summary: Kratos Defense & Security Solutions (KTOS) has secured a contract with Airbus Defence and Space to provide a ground segment for the OmanSat-1 satellite project. While the company demonstrates strong financial strength and a positive long-term stock trend, it faces issues with declining margins, insider selling, and potential inefficiencies in capital utilization. Investors are advised to be cautious despite the positive contract news.

feed.title: JKHY Sees Rating Upgrade and Price Target Increase by Wells Farg
feed.summary: Wells Fargo has upgraded Jack Henry & Associates (JKHY) to 'Overweight' from 'Equal-Weight', raising its price target to $196 from $181. This upgrade reflects increased confidence in the company's financial technology services and growth prospects for US banks and credit unions. Analysts forecast an average target price of $202.51 for JKHY, implying a significant upside from its current price.

feed.title: Invesco Sees Case for UK as Non-Tech Diversification Play
feed.summary: Invesco strategist Ben Gutteridge suggests UK equities are a valuable diversification play, offering an alternative to the tech-heavy US market. He cites potential benefits from upcoming interest rate cuts and discounted valuations for UK companies in sectors like energy, tobacco, and mining compared to their international counterparts. The article highlights the UK market as a potential safe haven for investors seeking to broaden their portfolios.

feed.title: Exxon Mobil (XOM) Fined A$16M for Misleading Fuel Claims in Aust
feed.summary: Exxon Mobil's Australian subsidiary, Mobil Oil Australia, has been fined A$16 million by the Federal Court for making misleading claims about additives in its "Mobil Synergy Fuel" between August 2020 and July 2024. The energy giant, with a robust financial standing and strong market capitalization, acknowledged the deceptive practices. Despite this legal challenge, Exxon Mobil maintains solid profitability and a healthy balance sheet, though insider selling and high valuation metrics are noted concerns.

feed.title: WYNN: Mizuho Raises Price Target to $134, Maintains Outperform R
feed.summary: Mizuho has raised its price target for Wynn Resorts (WYNN) to $134, up from $131, while maintaining an Outperform rating. This adjustment reflects varying analyst perspectives, with other firms also adjusting targets and ratings for WYNN recently. The consensus across 18 analysts sets an average target price of $143.03, suggesting a 26.15% upside from its current price of $113.38, and the average brokerage recommendation indicates an "Outperform" status.

feed.title: Vulcan’s Earnings Are Dragging Down Construction Materials Stocks.
feed.summary: Vulcan Materials' recent earnings report, while showing improvement, did not meet investor expectations, leading to downward pressure on other construction materials stocks. The company's performance is influencing the broader sector.

feed.title: Mariner, State Street to scale operations via Charles River platform
feed.summary: Mariner and State Street have partnered to implement Charles River Development's wealth management solution. This collaboration aims to scale Mariner's operations, supporting its expansion towards 5,000 advisers. Charles River Development, a subsidiary of State Street, provides the financial technology platform for this initiative.

feed.title: Dime Awards Grant to LIEOC for Community Services
feed.summary: Dime Community Bancshares, Inc. announced a grant to the Long Island Economic Opportunity Collaborative (LIEOC). This grant will support academic and vocational training initiatives aimed at promoting personal growth, career advancement, and social mobility services in Nassau and Suffolk counties. Dime Community Bank is a significant financial institution on Long Island with approximately $15 billion in assets.

feed.title: ABS veteran Christopher Wiernicki joins Royal Caribbean board
feed.summary: Christopher Wiernicki, former chairman and CEO of the American Bureau of Shipping, has been appointed to Royal Caribbean Group's board of directors, increasing the board size to 14. Wiernicki brings over four decades of marine industry expertise in design, operations, safety, digitalization, cybersecurity, and clean energy transition. Royal Caribbean Group Chairman/CEO Jason Liberty highlighted Wiernicki's disciplined approach and deep marine industry knowledge as valuable assets for the company's responsible growth as a global vacation leader.

feed.title: Kinross Gold Stock Rallies 38% in 3 Months: What Should Investors Do?
feed.summary: Kinross Gold Corporation (KGC) shares have surged 38.1% in the past three months, driven by rising gold prices and outperforming its industry peers. The company has a strong production profile with promising development projects expected to boost production and cash flow, along with solid financial health allowing for debt repayment and shareholder returns. Despite higher production costs, rising earnings estimates and a favorable gold price environment suggest a "Hold" recommendation for current investors.
